description: Programming language lineage graph v4. 112 languages, 300 relationships.

languages.name: Machine Code
languages.notes: Root node: binary instructions executed directly by CPU. Foundation of all bootstrap chains.

languages.name: Assembly
languages.notes: Low-level language with direct CPU instruction correspondence. First assembler ~1949.

languages.name: Fortran
languages.notes: Oldest high-level language still in use. IBM. First compiler written in assembly, modern compilers self-hosting.

languages.name: Lisp
languages.notes: John McCarthy. Second-oldest high-level language still used. Eval written in Lisp itself early on.

languages.name: COBOL
languages.notes: English-like business language. First compiler 1960. Still runs massive financial systems.

languages.name: ALGOL
languages.notes: ALGOL 58/60. Hugely influential. Inspired Pascal, C, and most imperative languages.

languages.name: Simula
languages.notes: First OOP language. Introduced classes, objects, inheritance. Inspired Smalltalk and C++.

languages.name: BCPL
languages.notes: Basic Combined Programming Language. Martin Richards. Direct ancestor of B and C.

languages.name: B
languages.notes: Ken Thompson at Bell Labs. Derived from BCPL. Direct predecessor of C.

languages.name: Pascal
languages.notes: Niklaus Wirth. P-code portable compiler bootstrapped itself. Influenced Ada and Delphi.

languages.name: Smalltalk
languages.notes: Xerox PARC. Alan Kay, Dan Ingalls, Adele Goldberg. Smalltalk-80 partially self-hosting.

languages.name: C
languages.notes: Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs. Bootstrapped from B. Self-hosting within first year. Foundation for dozens of languages.

languages.name: ML
languages.notes: Robin Milner at Edinburgh. First language with Hindley-Milner type inference. Ancestor of OCaml, SML, Haskell, F#.

languages.name: Scheme
languages.notes: Sussman and Steele. Minimalist Lisp dialect. Proper tail calls, first-class continuations.

languages.name: Prolog
languages.notes: Colmerauer and Roussel. SWI-Prolog written in C.

languages.name: Ada
languages.notes: DoD-commissioned. GNAT compiler (GCC-based) is self-hosting. Safety-critical systems.

languages.name: C++
languages.notes: Bjarne Stroustrup. First compiler (Cfront) translated C++ to C. GCC and Clang now primary implementations.

languages.name: Objective-C
languages.notes: Brad Cox and Tom Love. Smalltalk messaging added to C. Apple platform language until Swift.

languages.name: Erlang
languages.notes: Ericsson. BEAM VM. Compiler self-hosting since ~1991. BEAM VM written in C.

languages.name: Perl
languages.notes: Larry Wall. First release Dec 18 1987. Text processing and sysadmin. Influenced PHP and Ruby.

languages.name: Standard ML
languages.notes: Standardized ML dialect. SML/NJ self-hosting. MLton whole-program compiler also in SML.

languages.name: Haskell
languages.notes: Pure lazy functional. GHC self-hosting written in Haskell. RTS written in C. Named after Haskell Curry.

languages.name: Python
languages.notes: Guido van Rossum. CPython in C. PyPy alternative in RPython. Not self-hosting in standard distribution.

languages.name: Common Lisp
languages.notes: ANSI standardized Lisp. SBCL self-hosting. CLOS object system included.

languages.name: OCaml
languages.notes: INRIA. Self-hosting compiler. Runtime in C and Assembly. Original Rust compiler (rustboot) written in OCaml.

languages.name: Java
languages.notes: Sun Microsystems. javac self-hosting. HotSpot JVM in C++. Write-once-run-anywhere via JVM bytecode.

languages.name: JavaScript
languages.notes: Brendan Eich at Netscape. Influenced by Self and Scheme. V8/SpiderMonkey/JavaScriptCore in C++.

languages.name: Ruby
languages.notes: Yukihiro Matsumoto. MRI/CRuby in C. JRuby on JVM. Influenced by Smalltalk, Perl, Python.

languages.name: PHP
languages.notes: Rasmus Lerdorf. Zend Engine in C. Influenced by Perl and C.

languages.name: R
languages.notes: Ross Ihaka and Robert Gentleman. Statistical computing. C and Fortran implementation.

languages.name: Racket
languages.notes: Originally PLT Scheme. Transitioned backend to Chez Scheme in 2019. Self-hosting.

languages.name: C#
languages.notes: Microsoft. Anders Hejlsberg. Roslyn (self-hosting since 2014) replaced earlier C++ compiler.

languages.name: Lua
languages.notes: PUC-Rio. Written in ANSI C. LuaJIT JIT implementation also in C. Designed for embedding.

languages.name: D
languages.notes: Walter Bright. DMD reference compiler now written in D (bootstrapped from C++). Influenced by C++, Java, Python.

languages.name: Scala
languages.notes: Martin Odersky at EPFL. Scala 3 (Dotty) bootstrapped. Runs on JVM. Combines OOP and FP.

languages.name: F#
languages.notes: Don Syme at Microsoft Research. Derived from OCaml. Initial compiler in OCaml, rewritten in F# for self-hosting. Runs on .NET.

languages.name: Clojure
languages.notes: Rich Hickey. Lisp dialect for JVM. Compiler in Java (intentional). ClojureScript compiles to JS.

languages.name: Go
languages.notes: Google. Rob Pike, Ken Thompson, Robert Griesemer. Original compiler in C, rewritten in Go in version 1.5 (2015).

languages.name: CoffeeScript
languages.notes: Jeremy Ashkenas. Transpiles to JavaScript. Self-hosting compiler. Influenced by Python, Ruby, Haskell.

languages.name: Rust
languages.notes: Mozilla. Graydon Hoare. Original compiler (rustboot) in OCaml. Rewritten in Rust ~2011. LLVM backend.

languages.name: Kotlin
languages.notes: JetBrains. First compiler in Java, self-hosting. K2 compiler frontend released 2024. JVM, JS, Native targets.

languages.name: TypeScript
languages.notes: Microsoft. Self-hosting tsc. TypeScript 7.0 will rewrite compiler in Go for 10x performance.

languages.name: Dart
languages.notes: Google. Dart SDK and VM in C++. Native, JS, WASM targets. Primary language for Flutter.

languages.name: Elixir
languages.notes: José Valim. Runs on BEAM. Bootstrap compiler in Erlang, then self-hosting. Deliberately keeps Erlang bootstrap.

languages.name: Elm
languages.notes: Evan Czaplicki. Compiler written in Haskell. Compiles to JavaScript.

languages.name: Julia
languages.notes: MIT. Jeff Bezanson et al. LLVM JIT. Parser originally in FemtoLisp, transitioning to Julia as of v1.10.

languages.name: Swift
languages.notes: Apple. Chris Lattner. Original compiler in C++/LLVM. Significant parts now in Swift. Contested self-hosting status.

languages.name: Crystal
languages.notes: Ruby-inspired syntax with static typing. Bootstrapped from Ruby. Fully self-hosting.

languages.name: PureScript
languages.notes: Phil Freeman. Compiler in Haskell. Compiles to JavaScript.

languages.name: Nim
languages.notes: Andreas Rumpf. Self-hosting from first release year (2008). Transpiles to C/C++/JS. Influenced by Python, Ada, Modula.

languages.name: ReasonML
languages.notes: Facebook/Meta. Alternative syntax for OCaml. Tooling in OCaml. Influenced by JavaScript and OCaml.

languages.name: Zig
languages.notes: Andrew Kelley. Original compiler in C++. Self-hosting 2022. Dropped C++ implementation. Own codegen option.

languages.name: V
languages.notes: Alexander Medvednikov. Influenced by Go. Self-hosting compiler. Transpiles to C.

languages.name: Carbon
languages.notes: Google. Experimental C++ successor. Compiler in C++. LLVM backend. Not yet self-hosting.

languages.name: Mojo
languages.notes: Modular AI. Python-superset for AI/ML. MLIR/LLVM backend. Not yet self-hosting.

languages.name: Groovy
languages.notes: Apache. JVM language. Compiler intentionally in Java. Influenced by Python, Ruby, Smalltalk.

languages.name: Visual Basic .NET
languages.notes: Microsoft. Roslyn handles both C# and VB.NET. Runs on .NET CLR.

languages.name: GCC
languages.notes: GNU Compiler Collection. Originally in C. Switched to C++ in 2012. Self-hosting.

languages.name: LLVM
languages.notes: Low Level Virtual Machine. Chris Lattner. Backend for Rust, Swift, Crystal, Zig, Julia, Clang.

languages.name: Clang
languages.notes: LLVM C/C++/Objective-C frontend. Written in C++. Apple-backed.

languages.name: HotSpot JVM
languages.notes: Sun/Oracle JVM. C++. JIT compiler. OpenJDK primary JVM.

languages.name: .NET Runtime
languages.notes: CoreCLR. Microsoft. C++ and Assembly. Open-sourced 2014.

languages.name: Roslyn
languages.notes: .NET Compiler Platform. Handles C# and VB.NET. Rewrote C++ compiler in C#. Open-sourced 2014.

languages.name: V8
languages.notes: Google JavaScript engine. Powers Chrome and Node.js. Written in C++.

languages.name: SpiderMonkey
languages.notes: Mozilla JavaScript engine. Original C by Brendan Eich. Now C++ and Rust.

languages.name: JavaScriptCore
languages.notes: Apple/WebKit JavaScript engine. C++ and Assembly. Powers Safari.

languages.name: GHC
languages.notes: Glasgow Haskell Compiler. Primary Haskell implementation. Self-hosting. RTS in C.

languages.name: mrustc
languages.notes: Alternative Rust compiler in C++. Used for bootstrapping Rust from non-Rust sources.

languages.name: BEAM VM
languages.notes: Bogdan/Björn's Erlang Abstract Machine. Written in C. Runs Erlang, Elixir, Gleam, LFE.

languages.name: Chez Scheme
languages.notes: R. Kent Dybvig. Self-hosting Scheme compiler using nanopass framework. Now powers Racket backend.

languages.name: FemtoLisp
languages.notes: Lightweight Scheme-like Lisp by Jeff Bezanson. Used as Julia's parser frontend until v1.10.

languages.name: FLOW-MATIC
languages.notes: First English-like data processing language, direct predecessor to COBOL

languages.name: COMTRAN
languages.notes: IBM's Commercial Translator, co-predecessor to COBOL

languages.name: Planner
languages.notes: Logic programming precursor that directly influenced Prolog's design

languages.name: Lazy ML
languages.notes: Lazy functional language used as initial implementation language for GHC prototype (1989)

languages.name: Newsqueak
languages.notes: Concurrent programming language by Rob Pike, influenced Go's concurrency model

languages.name: Limbo
languages.notes: Systems programming language for the Inferno OS, influenced Go

languages.name: Oberon-2
languages.notes: Extension of Oberon with type-bound procedures, influenced Go

languages.name: Alef
languages.notes: Concurrent systems language for Plan 9, influenced Go and Rust

languages.name: Cyclone
languages.notes: Safe dialect of C, influenced Rust's ownership and region-based memory system

languages.name: CLU
languages.notes: Pioneer in data abstraction, influenced iterators and exception handling in many languages including Rust

languages.name: Gleam
languages.notes: Functional language for Erlang VM, influenced by Rust

languages.name: Oberon
languages.notes: Successor to Modula-2 by Niklaus Wirth, influenced Go

languages.name: ABC
languages.notes: Teaching language developed at CWI, primary inspiration for Python

languages.name: Modula-3
languages.notes: Systems programming language, influenced Python's module and exception system

languages.name: Icon
languages.notes: High-level language with goal-directed evaluation, influenced Python's generators

languages.name: Dylan
languages.notes: Dynamic language influenced Python's object model

languages.name: Modula-2
languages.notes: Successor to Pascal with modules, influenced Go and many systems languages

languages.name: Self
languages.notes: Prototype-based OOP language, directly influenced JavaScript's object model

languages.name: Miranda
languages.notes: Lazy functional language that directly inspired Haskell

languages.name: MATLAB
languages.notes: Matrix-oriented language for scientific computing, influenced Julia

languages.name: Idris
languages.notes: Dependently typed functional language inspired by Haskell

languages.name: Agda
languages.notes: Dependently typed proof assistant and programming language

languages.name: Cython
languages.notes: Superset of Python that compiles to C for performance

languages.name: APL
languages.notes: Array programming language with concise notation, influenced many scientific languages

languages.name: Delphi
languages.notes: Object Pascal dialect for RAD, predecessor to C# design patterns

languages.name: J
languages.notes: Successor to APL by its creator, using ASCII characters

languages.name: Mercury
languages.notes: Purely declarative logic language, statically typed descendant of Prolog

languages.name: ALGOL 68
languages.notes: Revision of ALGOL 60, influenced C and Python

languages.name: PL/I
languages.notes: General-purpose IBM language combining Fortran and COBOL features

languages.name: Bash
languages.notes: Unix shell and scripting language, GNU replacement for Bourne shell

languages.name: Tcl
languages.notes: Scripting language for embedding in applications

languages.name: AWK
languages.notes: Text processing language, influenced Perl

languages.name: Bourne Shell
languages.notes: Original Unix shell, predecessor to Bash

languages.name: Angular
languages.notes: TypeScript-based web framework by Google

languages.name: LuaJIT
languages.notes: JIT compiler for Lua, significantly faster than standard Lua

languages.name: S
languages.notes: Statistical computing language, inspired R

languages.name: Pharo
languages.notes: Modern Smalltalk dialect focused on live programming

languages.name: GNU Guile
languages.notes: GNU's official scripting language, a Scheme dialect

languages.name: mypy
languages.notes: Optional static type checker for Python

languages.name: Free Pascal
languages.notes: Open-source Pascal and Object Pascal compiler

languages.name: Fortran 95
languages.notes: Revised Fortran standard with improved features

Elixir bootstrap compiler written in Erlang

tsc is self-hosting, written in TypeScript

C# Roslyn compiler written in C# (self-hosting)
Managed parts of CoreCLR written in C#

Swift compiler increasingly written in Swift
Swift designed as Objective-C successor for Apple
Minor influence on C++ OOP model

Zig compiler rewritten from C++ to Zig

Racket migrated backend to Chez Scheme in 2019

IBM's COMTRAN contributed data description concepts to COBOL design
FORTRAN influenced COBOL's arithmetic and data handling capabilities

GHC's runtime system (RTS) is written in C and C--
Planner (Carl Hewitt, 1969) directly inspired Prolog's logic programming paradigm; listed as influence on Wikipedia
Original GHC prototype written in Lazy ML by Kevin Hammond in 1989 before the Haskell rewrite

Smalltalk influenced Go's interface system

Scheme influenced Rust's macro and metaprogramming system
